steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to describe how scientific understanding about the glass’s origin has “evolved.” Evolution of understanding requires showing a change over time—an earlier explanation and a later, different explanation—so the reader can see that thinking shifted.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Mineral signatures of glass samples are consistent with those of comet samples collected by NASA, according to new research.’: Presents only the 2021 finding. With no earlier explanation to contrast it against, no evolution of understanding is shown—this reads as a single static fact. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘A 2017 study concluded that ancient grass fires had caused the glass’s formation, but new research suggests that the glass formed as a result of a cometary explosion close to the desert’s surface.’: Gives the 2017 explanation first, then uses “but” to introduce the 2021 finding as a contrasting update. Both time periods and the shift between them are explicit—understanding is shown evolving. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Scientists have long sought to determine the origin of the glass, with one study concluding that it formed when ancient grass fires melted the area’s sandy soil.’: Mentions only the grass-fire study and frames it as an ongoing search. The 2021 cometary finding is omitted entirely, so no change in understanding is shown. ✗
  • Choice D: Original text is unavailable in source data. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
